internal.description: Self-referencing FK to the original evaluation an override row supersedes. Restrict on delete so the audit chain cannot be broken silently.

outbound.description: The admin who approved an override. Set only on override rows.
Polymorphic pointer to WHY the evaluation happened (the caller's entity — staff clock-in, attendance record, reception visit). Resolved via the morph map. FK integrity enforced application-side.

Polymorphic pointer to WHAT the evaluation ran AGAINST (the fenced entity — branch, facility, venue, event, ...). Any model implementing the Geofenceable interface. Resolved via the morph map. FK integrity enforced application-side.

inbound.description: Consuming modules (staff-clock-in-out, athlete-self-check-in, visitor-auto-log, attendance) stamp `geofence_check_id` on their own record. Those FKs are OWNED BY THE CONSUMER, not by this module. Fenceable models (branch, facility, venue, ...) receive an inbound `geofenceChecks(): MorphMany` relation from the HasGeofence trait for querying their own audit history.

morph_map_registration.description: Two morph pairs — two alias namespaces. Consuming modules register both via attributes discovered at boot.
